source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The Wanderer
archetype.receipt: top three heroes are only 36% of 755 games — spreading games this thin slows the climb in every queue.

entries.change: Move the midlane games to carry
Queue carry instead of the usual mix
entries.receipt: 55.1% there vs 51.4% overall, 172 games of evidence
52.6% over 95 games on Serath vs 51.4% overall

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

pool.name: Serath
pool.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +14.7% on the burst combo at minute 15.

leanInto.name: Serath
leanInto.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +14.7% on the burst combo at minute 15.

plan: Queue carry as primary. It is Cuban's best role at 55.1% over 172 games (overall is 51.4%). Set midlane as secondary, not the other way around.
Stop queueing midlane: 45.8% over 201 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play the safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Serath. Compared with the average player on the same hero, Cuban wins about 2.8 more games per 100 on it.
Cuban's champion pool is 10+ heroes wide and the top three are only 36% of the games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on the top three.
The math: Cuban is at 354 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. The all-time peak is 824.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward the player's own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where the player wins, not why

insights.title: Goldilocks324 brings out Cuban's best
insights.finding: Cuban's record alongside Goldilocks324 is 56% over 210 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 2 pentakills in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 21 (plus 19 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Cuban has receipts.

insights.title: Cuban's lean, relative to the squad: objective damage
insights.finding: Cuban's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is objective damage at 1.1x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.
